_meta.title: Nekozoku Shōgundō — Codex
_meta.note: Curated, reader-facing source of truth for the unlockable Codex. Reader-safe (no endgame/confidential material). Entries unlock at first appearance in the chapters. Lexicon terms live in glossary.json; the Codex tracks their discovery separately. Image paths are appended to the ImageKit base; verify any flagged path against your CDN.

entries.name: Queen Byakko
entries.bio: Queen of the Nekozoku. Sovereign Protector of the Galaxy, Slayer of Fahng, Savior of the Nine Capitals — and the list goes on. In public she rises to every occasion in perfectly measured demeanor; in private she is a faithful sister and the fixed point of a singular inner circle. Bears the paired weapon Ichi and Zero. Four ears — the royal bloodline.

entries.name: Princess Yuki
entries.bio: Princess of the Realm, wielder of Jinsei, Hero of Karkanda Forest, Champion of the Queen and General of the Frontier Armies. Her youth is no measure of her capability — she was born for this. Four ears, like her sister.

entries.name: Yamaneko
entries.bio: "Aka" Yama. Cousin to the Queen and Princess, and the highest-ranking male Nekojin. A fierce warrior and a brilliant general — he commands the Army by day and leads the Trio on covert operations. His weapon is beyond mysterious.

entries.name: Arashi
entries.bio: Weapons-master to House Nekojin — Bakeneko, and ancient. She knows history at the deepest levels and is wise beyond measure. Blind, and yet she sees more than most. Bearer of Kaze. It was she who was sent for the Trio, and she who carried all three off a burning world; she has never called them anything but hers, and never once aloud.

entries.name: Shiro
entries.bio: Bakeneko spymaster of the old school. White hair shot with pale blue, geisha-white makeup, a black-and-white kimono cut like a business suit. She keeps the realm's quietest victories — and reads what a careful hand leaves behind when it believes no one will ever come to look.

entries.name: Mu
entries.bio: Faithful family pet, with a remarkably long lifespan. Occupies the corners of rooms in the precise way Mu occupies spaces — small, still, apparently irrelevant. The slow blink is reserved.

entries.name: Hakase Ren
entries.bio: Usagizoku scholar and interim diplomat to the Nekozoku court, serving while a formal ambassador is sought. Librarian of Oumura — and so, the one who can read what was carried off when its shelves went dark.

entries.name: Mineko · "Mine"
entries.bio: Born blind — and where sight should sit, dimensional energy sits instead, so she reads the substrate of a room rather than seeing it. The Trio's only outward voice, and the still point her sisters' scattered speech gathers into words. Bears Techū, the Mind-bow: the arrow arrives, it does not travel. Two auburn tails. A rare twin-tailed Nekojin, raised by Arashi.

entries.name: Kikaneko · "Kika"
entries.bio: Born deaf — and into that silence comes dimensional resonance instead, so she hears the lie folded inside a record. She is one with an Ascended so wholly that her weapon needs no blade: Jigen-Jitsu, the empty-handed yielding-art. She never speaks aloud; when she does, more wisps rise from her tails, and only Mine hears the words. Two blonde tails. The hardest of the three to know.

entries.name: Iwaneko · "Iwa"
entries.bio: Born without a voice — so Yōgantō speaks for her, glowing when she does, and only Mine hears the words. The lava-greatsword does not cut; it ends. Two jet-black tails. Never gloomy without cause, and always intent on the last word — the sister whose weapon ends things does not waste the word that comes before it. A rare twin-tailed Nekojin, raised by Arashi.

entries.name: Isha Riki
entries.bio: Physician of the Mangusuzoku — a solitary, self-possessed people, born to teeth and quickness but never made to run in any pack. Demure rounded ears, a short full-furred tail; the markers are subtle.

entries.name: Kit Kiko
entries.bio: MajoDomo of the Royal House — the steward who has already accounted for whoever is about to arrive, so that arrivals feel less like surprises than like appointments the other party forgot making. She knows what is coming, and has already set a place for it.

entries.name: Vrach Roak
entries.bio: A healer of the court. (Bible entry pending — lore to be developed.)

entries.name: Alpha Rex
entries.bio: Alpha of the DarkPack. He knows he is right, and he will unmake the universe to prove it. The hand behind the silences on the frontier.

entries.name: Beta Kijo
entries.bio: Beta of the DarkPack. She earned her place on the battlefield, and in Rex's heart when he had one. Fearless and loyal — the DarkPack are her pups.

entries.name: Thinker Xort
entries.bio: Intelligence chief of the DarkPack — small, weasel-kind, with a dark eye-mask marking and a red cybernetic eye behind goggles. Cold, calculating, contemptuous. She already knows what you are going to do before you do.

entries.name: Speaker Vox
entries.bio: The DarkPack's propagandist. A true believer with an instinct for the howling point — a sycophant with a real gift, which is the most dangerous kind.

entries.name: General Duuk
entries.bio: Rex's Chancellor and most devoted commander. Mauve armor, paired sai, notoriously cruel to subjugated peoples — and a vocal advocate for overturning the Prey Species Edict.

entries.name: Kage
entries.bio: A Lycan Galactic Pack intelligence operative — Secret Police, Unit Three — assigned among the DarkPack. Black-and-acid-green field uniform.

entries.name: Ichi / Zero
entries.bio: Byakko's paired blades — physically identical, reading a teal-violet-white spectrum. Zero rides the left scabbard, Ichi the right; she draws by reaching for her own ears. Only Byakko knows which is which, and she has never told a soul.

entries.name: Jinsei
entries.bio: A three-meter curved no-dachi. In Flora mode it trails a green cellular dissolution; Fauna mode is not yet revealed. Like all the great weapons, Jinsei arrives in this dimension — it does not travel to its mark.

entries.name: Techū
entries.bio: Mine's bow, recalled to the far side of the threshold until it is needed. The arrow does not cross the space between — it simply arrives at its mark.

entries.name: Jigen-Jitsu
entries.bio: The one weapon among the great Nine that is no object at all. Kika is one with her Ascended so wholly that it lives in her body rather than in any blade — an empty-handed dimensional yielding-art that turns a force back down the line it came. She does not draw a weapon. She is one, in motion.

entries.name: Yōgantō
entries.lit: Japanese 溶岩刀, "lava blade" · the Spirit
entries.bio: Iwa's great lava-sword. It does not cut — it ends, in the fullest sense the word can hold. Its fissures are not cracks in matter but tears in the dimension behind the world. It glows when Iwa speaks.

entries.name: Kaze
entries.bio: Arashi's weapon — a double-ended tanto with opposing edges, one blade most often kept sheathed. The grandmaster's wind, in the hands of the one who taught everyone else how to hold what they carry.

entries.name: Mujo
entries.bio: Yamaneko's weapon, holding the poles of Heaven and Earth. Beyond mysterious, and beyond most reckoning — its full nature is not yet shown.

entries.name: Nekojin Trooper Blade
entries.bio: The standard energy melee blade carried by Shogunate military troopers.

entries.name: Nekojin Trooper Firearm
entries.bio: The standard projectile firearm carried by Shogunate military troopers.

entries.name: Shogunate Plate
entries.bio: The plate of the Shōgunate — white and gold for the Royal House, black for the line troopers, all of it bearing the clan mon. (Armor lore to be expanded.)

entries.name: The Throne Room
entries.bio: The amber-lit throne room of the Nekozoku Shōgunate — polished stone, the seat of the civilization, the cushion where Mu sits.

entries.name: The Intelligence Bureau
entries.bio: The briefing room of the Nekozoku Intelligence Bureau — where the network map resolves and re-resolves, and where the Trio take their places along the wall.

entries.name: Oumura
entries.bio: A system gone dark; once Usagizoku research territory, its great library of dimensional scholarship carried off rather than burned — and useless without the scholar who shelved it.

entries.name: Leerma
entries.bio: A system gone dark — the older, colder silence, and the place where the hand walking the frontier first grew careless enough to be caught.

entries.name: Kahvan-Da
entries.bio: A frontier waystation — scene of a theft carried out off the roads and without a trace, by a hand that spent its rarest coin to move a single thing by stealth.
